display.purpose: Hero headings, landing value props, feature headers. Never used for body or navigation.

body.purpose: All body copy, table cells, descriptions. Line length capped at 72ch for readability.

label.purpose: Form labels, badge text, timestamps, metadata. Use uppercase sparingly.

shadows.name: hover-raise
shadows.purpose: Cards and buttons on hover. A subtle lift that signals affordance.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (color, box-shadow).

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: Default transition duration for interactive states.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action button. Dark navy fill, white text, 6px radius.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #14213d; color: #f8fafc; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(20, 33, 61, 0.5); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary action button. Border-only, transparent fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #14213d; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 6px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #f1f5f9; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(20, 33, 61, 0.5); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text input with cloud border and 6px radius.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 36px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1.5px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 6px; background: #ffffff; color: #14213d; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: border-color 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; outline: none; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #64748b; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #14213d; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(20, 33, 61, 0.5); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Data container card. White fill, cloud border, 10px radius.
components.html:
Policy Summary

Your policy details appear here.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 10px; padding: 24px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; color: #14213d; } .ds-card-header { margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-card-content { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #64748b; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Status badge with cloud background and stone text.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px; background: #e2e8f0; color: #64748b; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.333; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }

narrative.overview: ClaimWise is the desk where insurance becomes clear — authoritative in its knowledge but frictionless in its use. The visual system is anchored in confidence and precision: deep navy ink on cool off-white paper, with sharp, readable typography and purposeful white space. This is a tool that respects the gravity of insurance documents while never feeling like the insurance company itself. The interface rejects the generic SaaS playbook. No purple-to-blue gradients, no glassmorphism, no side-stripe borders, no numbered section scaffolding. Instead, it leans on a restrained toolkit — one strong accent, one text hierarchy that works on sight, and a tonal layering system that conveys depth without excessive shadows.
keyCharacteristics: Authoritative but not bureaucratic — trust through precision, not ornament
Data-forward: analysis, comparisons, and policy text are the hero
Calm complexity: generous whitespace, progressive disclosure, clear scanning hierarchies
Dark mode as a genuine alternative, not an afterthought

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Teal is the only active accent. Primary actions, links, and success states use it. If something is teal, it's interactive or positive. Rare is the point.

rules.name: The No-Warm-Background Rule
rules.body: Paper stays cool (blue-cast, not cream). Warmth comes from Instrument Serif headings and human tone in copy, not from the canvas.

rules.name: The Instrument Rule
rules.body: Instrument Serif is reserved for display-scale headings only. Never use it for body copy, navigation, or buttons. Its rarity is what makes it authoritative.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. No shadows on cards, sidebars, or panels in their default state. Shadow is a response, not an aesthetic choice.

dos: Use the Ink-Paper-Midnight triad as your primary palette. These three carry 90% of the surface area.
Reserve Instrument Serif for display headings only. Its presence should feel like a headline in a serious publication.
Let data breathe. Analysis results, comparison tables, and policy text need generous padding and clear visual hierarchy.
Use teal for interactive elements — it's the single accent that signals 'you can click this.'
Comply with WCAG AA at minimum: body text ≥4.5:1 contrast, large text ≥3:1.
Honor reduced motion preferences — all animations must degrade gracefully to instant transitions.
donts: Use Inter as the only font — the palette calls for the Instrument Serif counterpoint.
Use gradient text, glassmorphism, or side-stripe borders. These are explicitly prohibited.
Use numbered section markers (01 / 02 / 03) as default scaffolding.
Put tiny uppercase tracked eyebrows above every section.
Create identical card grids (same-sized cards with icon + heading + text, repeated endlessly).
Use purple-to-blue gradients, bounce/elastic easing, or cream/beige backgrounds.
Wrap everything in cards or nest cards inside cards.
Use pure black or pure gray — always tint toward the brand hue.
